name: Charlie
Erker
bullets: Honors Mechanical Engineering student, GPA 3.88 — KU Class of 2028
Currently at Siemens Digital Industries Software, AD&T division
Co-founded KU's first consulting prep program; 1st place case competitor
Built and sold a patent-pending product from scratch as a founder

items.title: Siemens Digital Industries Software
items.description: Business Operations & Commercial Enablement Intern on the AD&T team — working under the VP of Strategy Execution & Operations in Charlotte, NC.

items.title: KU Consulting Scholars Program
items.description: Co-founded KU's first structured consulting prep program in June 2026 — built the curriculum, recruited the founding cohort, and locked in practitioner partnerships.

items.title: Case Competition Wins
items.description: 1st place at Best in the Midwest National Case Competition and UMSL International Business Case Competition. 3rd at Wisconsin Invitational (co-sponsored by McKinsey, Accenture & Deloitte).

bio: My name is Charlie Erker. I'm a Mechanical Engineering student at KU with a minor in Business, originally from St. Louis. Over the past few years I've followed my passion for solving engineering problems in a business context.

So far that's looked like rebuilding operations processes at Siemens, co-founding a consulting prep program from scratch, and winning national case competitions. I'm continually drawn to problems that sit at the intersection of analytical and strategic.
Outside of work I play drums, run, and am passionate about marine biology. I strive to always be curious in every area of my life.

experience.title: Business Operations Intern
experience.org: Siemens Digital Industries Software · May 2026 – Present
experience.description: Working directly under the VP of Strategy Execution & Operations on the Aerospace, Defense & Transportation team. Rebuilt the client handoff process after identifying gaps between sales and delivery; the framework is now used across the team. Built client-facing materials that help the sales team tell a clearer story to aerospace and defense buyers.

experience.title: Marketing Intern
experience.description: Campus trend-scouting ambassador through Champion's EMBER program. Analyzed consumer behavior and product usage patterns among college-aged audiences and translated findings into product and marketing recommendations shared across design, marketing, and brand management teams.

experience.title: Corporate Intern
experience.description: Rotated across Nooter Construction, Nooter Eriksen Engineers, and Wyatt over six weeks. Worked in field engineering, preconstruction estimating, and construction management — including time in Chicago and Philadelphia offices and on active job sites with rigging engineers and drafters.

experience.title: Founder
experience.description: Founded Buck-It, a portable ventilation system for sports bags — from initial concept and garage fabrication through prototyping, cost control, and retail sales. Managed the full product lifecycle and brought it to market at live events.

press.headline: 1st Place — Led end-to-end development of Depth IA, an AI-integrated K-12 education platform, from ideation through prototyping and go-to-market strategy.

press.source: Best in the Midwest National Case Competition
press.headline: 1st Place — KU's invitational national case competition, planned, hosted, and funded by the Case Competition Club.
1st Place — Competed and won as part of KU's national case competition team.

press.source: Wisconsin Invitational Consulting · McKinsey / Accenture / Deloitte
press.headline: 3rd Place — Competed against top university teams at the Wisconsin Invitational, co-sponsored by McKinsey, Accenture & Deloitte.

projects.title: KU Consulting Scholars Program
projects.description: Co-founded KU's first structured consulting prep program. Built the curriculum, recruited the founding cohort, and established partnerships with practitioners across consulting and strategy.

projects.title: Case Competition Club
projects.description: Manage firm relationships and facilitate KU's consulting network. Co-run the Best in the Midwest National Case Competition — an invitational planned, hosted, and funded by the club.

projects.title: SELF Engineering Leadership Fellows
projects.description: Led four community service engineering projects with $1,500 budgets each — managed nonprofit outreach, project scoping, and team execution. Also run KU Engineering's annual high school design competition.

contact.heading: Let's Talk
contact.body: I'm always open to interesting conversations — whether it's about an internship, a full-time role, or something you're building. Reach out directly.

footer.name: Charlie Erker
footer.note: Engineering & Business · University of Kansas · Class of 2028
